fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 71 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so when `len(recent) == limit` the function incorrectly returns `True` (allowed) instead of `False` (blocked).

spec.expr: len(recent__1) < limit or len(recent__1) > limit or (len(recent__1) == limit)

constraints.intent: When recent count equals limit, the branch condition must be true (i.e., >= limit should trigger the block path), but with > it is false.
When recent count equals limit, the guard should treat it as at-or-over limit, so the condition len(recent) >= limit must hold.
In the else branch (allowed path), recent count must be strictly less than limit; if equal to limit we should not reach here.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] is True and len(_filter_recent(timestamps, now - window)) >= limit)

constraints.intent: The function must never return allowed=True when recent send count is at or above the limit.
